返回日报
Hacker News #9

克劳德·法布尔提出了雅可比猜想的一个反例。

Claude Fable produced a counterexample to the Jacobian Conjecture

AI 作者 loubbrad 热度 787 回复 502 2026/07/20 10:51
打开原帖 打开外部链接 JSON

摘要

  • Theorem
  • Define (F=(F_1,F_2,F_3):\mathbb C^3\to\mathbb C^3) by [ \begin{aligned} F_1(x,y,z) &=(1+xy)^3z+y^2(1+xy)(4+3xy),\[2mm] F_2(x,y,z) &=y+3x(1+xy)^2z+3xy^2(4+3xy),\[2mm] F_3(x,y,z) &=2x-3x^2y-x^3z. \end{aligned} ] Then

命中 2 个 AI/科技关键词。

正文翻译

暂无正文

暂无原文

回复翻译 20

koito17 2026/07/20 15:02
十多年前，我记得遇到过一位博士后，他相信自己找到了一个非常接近雅可比猜想反例的东西。他和另一个人正在暴力穷举大约16个变量的多项式，每个多项式大约有80到700项，并使用二叉树来映射系数。

当时他们猜测，反例(P, Q)在max(deg(P), deg(Q))上的下界会达到200。

想到Claude Fable竟然能在7次多项式中找到反例，我觉得简直不可思议。我们确实进入了一个新时代。

A little over 10 years ago I remember meeting a postdoc who believed he had something close to a counterexample to the Jacobian Conjecture. He and another person was bruteforcing polynomials in about 16 variables, something like 80 - 700 terms each, using binary trees for mapping coefficients.

They were guessing, at the time, that the lower bound of a counterexample (P, Q) for max(deg(P), deg(Q)) would go up to 200.

To think that Claude Fable was able to find a counterexample in degree 7 is insane to me. We are truly in a new era.

楼层
NooneAtAll3 2026/07/21 05:00
你可能把双变量情形（确实已验证到150次以上）和三变量情形（即这个反例）搞混了
you might be confusing 2 variable case (which indeed was tested to 150+ degree) and 3 variable case (this counterexample)
楼层
zaik 2026/07/20 15:08
这个反例难道没有包含在他们的搜索空间中吗？
Would this counterexample not be included in their search space?
楼层
koito17 2026/07/20 15:16
他们研究的是16个变量，每个变量的次数大约是10。对于单纯的暴力穷举来说，这个搜索空间实在太大了。所以他们做了一些过滤，将搜索空间缩小到一个包含“可能反例”的集合。

当时还有一篇论文给出了该特定框架下可能反例的下界约为100。后来在https://arxiv.org/abs/2204.14178中提高到了108

我不太记得细节了，因为那是2015年的事，而且我当时并没有真正参与这项研究。就把这当作是我2015年听到的内容和今天记忆之间的一种“传话游戏”吧。

They were looking at 16 variables, degree of about 10 in each one. That search space is simply too big for a plain bruteforce. So they did some sort of filtering to reduce the search space to a pool containing "possible counterexamples".

There was also a paper giving a lower bound of about 100 for possible counterexamples in that particular framework. Later raised to 108 in https://arxiv.org/abs/2204.14178

I don't remember the details very well since this was back in 2015 and wasn't really involved in the research. Consider this to be some sort of telephone game between what I heard in 2015 and what I remember today.

楼层
jasonfarnon 2026/07/20 16:11
根据所描述的方法，他们可能是在2维空间中研究的，而这个反例是3维的
based on the approach described they were probably in dimension 2, this counterexample is 3 dimensional

    16个变量

    16 variables

antirez 2026/07/20 17:35
这么多年来那么多数学家努力尝试都失败了，现在Anthropic为了搞点公关就神奇地做到了？而且这还是发生在LLM取得各种数学胜利之后？你这里的逻辑真是让我无法理解。
So many mathematicians over the years tried hard and failed, but now Anthropic just for some PR magically did it? And this after LLMs obtaining different math wins? What is your logic here really escapes my understanding.
楼层
glimshe 2026/07/20 18:03
原帖那篇毫无逻辑的帖子凸显了当今AI领域（乃至一切事物）的极化现象。我能理解有人出于道德、成本效益或生产力方面的原因反对AI。但如今我们看到了太多极端分子发的“AI一无是处”的帖子。
The parent's absolutely nonsensical post highlights how polarized AI (as everything else) is today. I can understand someone being opposed to AI on moral, cost-benefit or productivity grounds. But we're seeing a lot of extremist "AI is good for nothing" posts out there nowadays.
楼层
pred_ 2026/07/20 19:58
我完全不觉得这毫无逻辑。作者和他的合作者看起来都是聪明人，所以他们很可能提供了非平凡的见解来引导LLM，但显然他的雇主有动机淡化他们所提供的任何个人贡献。

编辑：现在原帖不知为何被标记/删除了。你可以不同意他们的看法（说这是营销噱头可能有点过了），但我认为这个论点是站得住脚的，所以标记掉它似乎对讨论适得其反。

I don't think it is nonsensical at all. The author and his collaborator both appear to be bright people, so there's a good chance they had to offer non-trivial insights to guide the LLM, yet it's clearly in the interest of his employer to downplay whatever personal contribution they provided.

Edit: Now the OP is flagged/dead for some reason. You could disagree on their take (calling it a marketing stunt is maybe a bit much), but I think the argument is sound, so flagging seems counterproductive to the discussion.

楼层
zaptheimpaler 2026/07/21 07:21
众所周知，这个问题已经悬而未决数十年，且至少有少数人在认真尝试解决，如果在这种情况下还认为AI只发挥了微乎其微的作用，那根本算不上逻辑自洽的立场。
Believing that AI played a very small role while we know this problem was open for decades with at least a few people taking serious cracks at it is just not a coherent logical position.
楼层
soerxpso 2026/07/21 04:04
如果属实，这甚至丝毫没有削弱Fable的贡献。大批研究生曾获得过同样具有实质意义的见解，最终却一无所获。
That doesn't really even diminish the contribution from Fable, if true. Droves of grad students have been provided the same sorts of non-trivial insights and turned up no results.
楼层
glimshe 2026/07/20 20:59
我敢肯定，他们有充足的时间在没有LLM的情况下思考这些见解，多年来许多其他试图攻克这个问题的数学家也是如此。LLM究竟是仅仅充当了助手还是完全解决了问题，这并不重要，重要的是要承认LLM是解决方案中至关重要、此前一直缺失的一环。
I'm sure they had plenty of time to think about these insights without the LLM, as well as the many other mathematicians who tried to crack it over the years. Wether the LLM was simply an assistant or solved the problem entirely isn't as important as accepting than the LLM was the essential, previously missing piece in the solution.
楼层
witx 2026/07/21 13:47
这就是HN。根本不允许对大语言模型有任何批评
That's HN. No criticism allowed of llm whatsoever

minimaxir 2026/07/21 01:21
> 我认为这个论点是合理的

他们被标记是因为这是一个假设恶意的字面阴谋论。

> I think the argument is sound

They got flagged because it is a literal conspiracy theory that assumes bad faith.

latexr 2026/07/21 17:13
> 纯粹的阴谋论

他们用的最强烈的词不过是“营销噱头”。把这说成是阴谋论未免太牵强了。

> literal conspiracy theory

The strongest words they used were “marketing stunt”. Calling that a conspiracy theory is quite the stretch.

楼层
abc42 2026/07/21 02:37
如果大多数人只是乐在其中地使用这项技术，而少数人却不断散布妄想与仇恨，这还能叫两极分化吗？
If you can call it polarized when a majority of people are just happily using the technology while a minority keeps spreading delusions and hatred.
楼层
raxxorraxor 2026/07/20 19:21
那些愚蠢的广告商为了曝光率什么都干得出来，如果这意味着要死磕一个冷门的所谓数学定理来反驳它，那他们就会这么干！

当然，Claude是怎么处理这个问题的会非常有趣。可能会对输入施加一些限制。而这些限制具体是什么也会很有意思。

Those silly advertisers do everything for exposure and if that means digging yourself into a niche alleged mathematical theorem to refute it, it is what needs to be done!

Of course it would be really interesting how Claude approached this. Probably with some constraints regarding the input. And it would be interesting what these constraints were.

楼层
dist-epoch 2026/07/20 19:45
作者可能不想展示提示词，因为他们现在正试图用同一个提示词去寻找一堆其他的反例
Author probably doesn't want to show the prompt because they are now trying to find a bunch of other counter-examples with the same prompt
